visa_types.documents_required:
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photography). If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection
General information
- The customer is advised to provide all the necessary documentation in original. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.
- Please note that written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child must be submitted in original only. Certified copies are not accepted, the submitted original consent remains in the archive of the Consular Section and is not returned. To cross the border you will also need to provide original parental consent, so please pre-arrange receiving two original versions of this document from the notary office.
- Additional documentation may be requested by the Embassy of Greece.
- The following documents do not require translation or apostil.
- All submitted original documents except the passports remain in the archive of the Consular Section and are not returned.
General requirements:
1. Application form
- Completed and signed by the customer. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer. To download application form, please, visit the section <<Download Form>>.

- The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
- Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
- Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. To download Agreement, please, visit the section Download Form.

- For your convenience Visa Application Centre offers the additional service of visa application form filling. The cost of the service is UAH 85. You only need to provide information to the VAC employee who will fill in the data into an electronic form and attach a print-out to the supporting documents.

2. 1 Photograph
- In color, 3,5x4,5 cm
- Facing forward, white background
- Not older than 6 months before the submission date
- More detailed information can be found in the <<Photo specifications>> section
- You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5x4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.

3. International passport(s)
- The passport must satisfy all criteria enlisted in art 12 of the Visa Code. Must be valid at least 3 months after the planned trip. Passport must contain 2 blank pages marked <<visas>>.
- If a child is travelling with a parent's passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all the pages of international passport except the blank ones
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - customers under 18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
4. Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport except the blank ones
5. Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for customers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.

- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.

6. Minors that are travelling 
- Written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child. The written consent must have a stamp of a Notary public and has to be presented in original only. If there is only one custodian, a birth certificate, the court decision on sole custody, death certificate or other document must be presented to prove this. If the mother is entitled to sole custody in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine, you must provide a certificate from the Registry Office that a mother is raising a child alone in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - if one/both parents have a valid Schengen visa and do not apply for a Greek Schengen visa, it is necessary to provide a written consent where the parent/parents give their permission for a child to travel and apply for a visa.
- Copies of all pages of the international and national passport of both parents (except the blank ones).
- Work certificates and financial documents of both parents.
- Birth certificate - original and copy.
7. Tickets and accommodation 
- Reservation of tickets and booking of a hotel with the personal details of the travelers. If customers rent a yacht, they must provide: a yacht lease contract, confirmation of payment, a list of all passengers traveling on a cruise, including crew.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
8. Proof of activity 
- For employed persons: Employment certificate confirming the position of employee, the salary gained within the previous period of 6 months, the date of employment, and the duration of the vacation,
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - a copy of the work book must be provided.
- For private entrepreneurs: 
- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company's payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months. 
- For pupils: Original certificate from the educational institution, which states that it is issued for submission to the Embassy of Greece.
- For students: 
- Certificate from the university indicating the faculty, curriculum and graduation date, as well as the fact that the certificate is issued to the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine;
- A copy of the student card, certified by the university;
- A copy of the record book, certified by the university
9. Proof of sufficient financial means 
- A bank account statement for the last 3 months and a copy of the bank card,
- If applicable, the customer may present additional proof of personal means, such as proof of real estate property or proof of car ownership.
- Please, note that the minimum amount of funds in the account - 50 euros per day of stay in Greece for one person.
Sponsorship 
- Expenses may be covered by the host or other third persons. The document that proves other person covering such expenses shall be a statement approved by public Notary and proof of income of the sponsor.
- Sponsorship letter for the family members (husband, wife, children) may be hand-written or typed, at your discretion. You must also provide proof of family relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate). For people who are not relatives, (husband, wife or children), sponsorship letter must be notarized. In both cases, the sponsorship letters should be complemented by a copy of the information page of internal or foreign passport and a certificate, proof of solvency of the sponsor (certificate of employment with an extract of wages for the last 6 months and a certificate from the bank on the movement of funds in the account for the last 3 months).
- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
10. In case of tourism by the means of individual transport
- A detailed travel plan.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If customers rent a yacht, they must provide: a yacht lease contract, confirmation of payment, a list of all passengers traveling on a cruise, including crew.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members
11. For the customers travelling with their families: 
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

visa_types.processing_time:
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ==) option to find out current status of your application.
VFS Global has no say in visa decisions taken by Embassy and Consulate General of Greece in Kiev and Odessa.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Tourist category is intended for persons who wish to travel to Greece for the purpose of tourism and who have no inviting party in Greece. Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Checklist_Tourist_visit_090416.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of customers submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customer must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph). 
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

- If a child is travelling with a parent's passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all the pages of international passport except the blank ones.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - customers under 18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
- Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport except the blank ones
- Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for customers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.
- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.
- Minors that are travelling
- Written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child. The written consent must have a stamp of a Notary public and has to be presented in original. If there is only one custodian, a birth certificate, the court decision on sole custody, death certificate or other document must be presented to prove this. If the mother is entitled to sole custody in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine, you must provide a certificate from the Registry Office that a mother is raising a child alone in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - if one/both parents have a valid Schengen visa and do not apply for a Greek Schengen visa, it is necessary to provide a written consent where the parent/parents give their permission for a child to travel and apply for a visa.
- Copies of all pages of the international and national passport of both parents (except the blank ones).
- Work certificates and financial documents of both parents
- Birth certificate - original and copy.
- Proof of activity
- For employed persons
: Employment certificate confirming the position of employee, the salary gained within the previous period of 6 months, the date of employment, and the duration of the vacation,
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - a copy of the work book must be provided.
- For private entrepreneurs:
- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company's payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners:
 Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- For pupils:
 Original certificate from the educational institution, which states that it is issued for submission to the Embassy of Greece.
- For students:
- Certificate from the university indicating the faculty, curriculum and graduation date, as well as the fact that the certificate is issued to the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine;
- A copy of the student card, certified by the university;
- A copy of the record book, certified by the university
- Proof of sufficient financial means
- A bank account statement for the last 3 months and a copy of the bank card,
- If applicable, the customer may present additional proof of personal means, such as proof of real estate property or proof of car ownership
- Please, note that the minimum amount of funds in the account - 50 euros per day of stay in Greece for one person.
Sponsorship Expenses may be covered by the host or other third persons. If the host company is covering all the trip related expenses, the fact should be mentioned in the invitation letter.
- Sponsorship letter for the family members (husband, wife, children) may be hand-written or typed, at your discretion. You must also provide proof of family relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate). For people who are not relatives, (husband, wife or children), sponsorship letter must be notarized. In both cases, the sponsorship letters should be complemented by a copy of the information page of internal or foreign passport and a certificate, proof of solvency of the sponsor (certificate of employment with an extract of wages for the last 6 months and a certificate from the bank on the movement of funds in the account for the last 3 months).
- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
2. Written request (invitation) from host organization.Invitation/confirmation in accordance with VFA art 4 (1) (h). The invitation should be issued on the letterhead of the host organization, stamped and signed by the authorized person. The invitation shall indicate the passport data of the invited citizen of Ukraine, namely: name, surname, date of birth, gender, nationality, passport number; time and purpose of the trip, number of entries, duration of stay. If the inviting party will cover the expenses or provide a residence for the period of travel, this fact should also be reflected in the invitation.
3. In case of visit of fares/conferences
- Proof of registration or a copy of a ticket.
4. Tickets and accommodation
- Booking of the flight tickets.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If details of the accommodation during the trip are not specified in the invitation, the customer should provide a separate hotel booking.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
5. For the customers travelling with their families:
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

visa_types.processing_time:
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ==) option to find out current status of your application.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who wish to travel to Greece in order to attend a Conference, Cultural or Sports Event for maximum of 90 days within 6 months.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Conference_Culture_Sports_event_040815.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph).
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who wish to travel to Greece for business purposes (i.e.: business meeting,) for maximum of 90 days within 6 months.Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/business_people_040815.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the “Documents Required” section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph).
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa – application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 – 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

[Table:   Business Visa]
Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined by the Visa Code of EU. All paid visa fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.

## Documents Required
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photography). If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection
General information
- The customer is advised to provide all the necessary documentation in original. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.
- Please note that written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child must be submitted in original only. Certified copies are not accepted, the submitted original consent remains in the archive of the Consular Section and is not returned. To cross the border you will also need to provide original parental consent, so please pre-arrange receiving two original versions of this document from the notary office.
- Additional documentation may be requested by the Embassy of Greece.
- The following documents do not require translation or apostil.
- All submitted original documents except the passports remain in the archive of the Consular Section and are not returned.
General requirements:
- Application form
- Completed and signed by the customer. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer. To download application form, please, visit the section “Download Form”.
- The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
- Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
- Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. To download Agreement, please, visit the section Download Form.
- For your convenience Visa Application Centre offers the additional service of visa application form filling. The cost of the service is UAH 85. You only need to provide information to the VAC employee who will fill in the data into an electronic form and attach a print-out to the supporting documents.
- 1 Photograph
- In color, 3,5x4,5 cm
- Facing forward, white background
- Not older than 6 months before the submission date
- More detailed information can be found in the “Photo specifications” section
- You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5x4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.
- International passport(s)
- The passport must satisfy all criteria enlisted in art 12 of the Visa Code. Must be valid at least 3 months after the planned trip. Passport must contain 2 blank pages marked “visas”.
- If a child is travelling with a parent’s passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all the pages of international passport from except the blank ones.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa – customers under18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
- Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport from 1 to 16 except the blank ones.
- Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for customers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.
- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.
- Minors that are travelling
- Written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child. The written consent must have a stamp of a Notary public and has to be presented in original. If there is only one custodian, a birth certificate, the court decision on sole custody, death certificate or other document must be presented to prove this. If the mother is entitled to sole custody in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine, you must provide a certificate from the Registry Office that a mother is raising a child alone in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa – if one/both parents have a valid Schengen visa and do not apply for a Greek Schengen visa, it is necessary to provide a written consent where the parent/parents give their permission for a child to travel and apply for a visa.

- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa – a copy of the work book must be provided.

- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company’s payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months.

- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
9. Written request (invitation) from the host company. According to art. 4 (1) (b) of the VFA. ## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes

In both cases, the sponsorship letters should be complemented by a copy of the information page of internal or foreign passport and a certificate, proof of solvency of the sponsor (certificate of employment with an extract of wages for the last 6 months and a certificate from the bank on the movement of funds in the account for the last 3 months).
- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
9. Transport 
- Booking of the flight tickets.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
10. Invitation 
- Invitation from the Greek national or Greek legal resident, certified by the Greek Authorities (police or citizens service centre), on the official letterhead, with a stamp and a signature of the authorized person.
- Copy of the internal passport of the Greek citizen or Residence card
- The most recent tax declaration of the inviting person
- If an customer is invited by the relative, all marriage and birth certificate copies must be submitted to prove their family ties.
11. For the customers travelling with their families:
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who wish to travel to Greece for the purpose of visiting Family or friends for maximum of 90 days within 6 months.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Private_visit_040815.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph).
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

## Overview
For persons who wish to travel to Greece for the purpose of visiting Family or friends for maximum of 90 days within 6 months.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Private_visit_040815.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph).
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Visit To Family Or Friends]
Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined by the Visa Code of EU. All paid visa fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.
To view the fees for other available additional services, please, click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ukr/en/grc/additional-services).

## Documents Required
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photography). If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection
General information
- The customer is advised to provide all the necessary documentation in original. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.

- Please note that written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child must be submitted in original only. Certified copies are not accepted, the submitted original consent remains in the archive of the Consular Section and is not returned. To cross the border you will also need to provide original parental consent, so please pre-arrange receiving two original versions of this document from the notary office.
- Additional documentation may be requested by the Embassy of Greece.
- The following documents do not require translation or apostil.
- All submitted original documents except the passports remain in the archive of the Consular Section and are not returned.
General requirements:
1. Application form 
- Completed and signed by the customer. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer. To download application form, please, visit the section <<Download Form>>.
- The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
- Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
- Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. To download Agreement, please, visit the section Download Form.
- For your convenience Visa Application Centre offers the additional service of visa application form filling. The cost of the service is UAH 85. You only need to provide information to the VAC employee who will fill in the data into an electronic form and attach a print-out to the supporting documents.
2. 1 Photograph 
- In color, 3,5x4,5 cm
- Facing forward, white background
- Not older than 6 months before the submission date
- More detailed information can be found in the <<Photo specifications>> section
- You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5x4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.
3. International passport(s)
- The passport must satisfy all criteria enlisted in art 12 of the Visa Code. Must be valid at least 3 months after the planned trip. Passport must contain 2 blank pages marked <<visas>>.
- If a child is travelling with a parent's passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all pages of international passport except the blank ones
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - customers  under 18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
4. Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport except the blank ones
5. Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for customers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.

- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.

6. Minors that are travelling
- Written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child. The written consent must have a stamp of a Notary public and has to be presented in original. If there is only one custodian, a birth certificate, the court decision on sole custody, death certificate or other document must be presented to prove this. If the mother is entitled to sole custody in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine, you must provide a certificate from the Registry Office that a mother is raising a child alone in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - if one/both parents have a valid Schengen visa and do not apply for a Greek Schengen visa, it is necessary to provide a written consent where the parent/parents give their permission for a child to travel and apply for a visa.
- Copies of all pages of the international and national passport of both parents (except the blank ones).
- Work certificates and financial documents of both parents
- Birth certificate - original and copy.
7. Proof of activity
- For employed persons: Employment certificate confirming the position of employee, the salary gained within the previous period of 6 months, the date of employment, and the duration of the vacation,
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - a copy  of the work book must be provided.
- For private entrepreneurs: 
- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company's payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- For pupils: Original certificate from the educational institution, which states that it is issued for submission to the Embassy of Greece.
- For students: 
- Certificate from the university indicating the faculty, curriculum and graduation date, as well as the fact that the certificate is issued to the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine;
- A copy of the student card, certified by the university;
- A copy of the record book, certified by the university
8. Proof of sufficient financial means
- A bank account statement for the last 3 months and a copy of the bank card,
- If applicable, the customer may present additional proof of personal means, such as proof of real estate property or proof of car ownership.
- Please, note that the minimum amount of funds in the account - 50 euros per day of stay in Greece for one person.
Sponsorship
- Expenses may be covered by the host or other third persons.
- In case the host covers the expenses, he/she should mention this fact in the invitation. Proof of the income of the sponsor is also necessary (e.g. tax statement, ownership of property etc.)
- The document that proves other person covering such expenses shall be a statement approved by public Notary and proof of income of the sponsor.
- Sponsorship letter for the family members (husband, wife, children) may be hand-written or typed, at your discretion. You must also provide proof of family relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate). For people who are not relatives, (husband, wife or children), sponsorship letter must be notarized. In both cases, the sponsorship letters should be complemented by a copy of the information page of internal or foreign passport and a certificate, proof of solvency of the sponsor (certificate of employment with an extract of wages for the last 6 months and a certificate from the bank on the movement of funds in the account for the last 3 months).
- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
9. Transport 
- Booking of the flight tickets.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
10. Invitation 
- Invitation from the Greek national or Greek legal resident, certified by the Greek Authorities (police or citizens service centre), on the official letterhead, with a stamp and a signature of the authorized person.
- Copy of the internal passport of the Greek citizen or Residence card
- The most recent tax declaration of the inviting person
- If an customer is invited by the relative, all marriage and birth certificate copies must be submitted to prove their family ties.
11. For the customers travelling with their families:
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5 * 4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.

## Processing Time
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ==) option to find out current status of your application.
VFS Global has no say in visa decisions taken by Embassy and Consulate General of Greece in Kiev and Odessa.

## Download Forms
Effective 26 May 2014 the Visa application Centre introduced the Form Filling service for the convenience of the customers at the centre.
 
The service is purely optional and the customers may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH 85 (all taxes included).

Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.
Forms listed below must be filled in completely and must be signed by the customer himself/herself. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer.
You can find an example of filled in visa application form here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/aitisiukrainan_040815.pdf)
The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. Please click here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/new-agrement-pilgrimage.pdf) to download the blank of the Personal Data Information Agreement

## Overview
For persons who wish to travel to Greece for medical treatment for maximum of 90 days within 6 months. Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Medical_treatment_040815.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, Customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph). 
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Medical Treatment]
Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined by the Visa Code of EU. All paid visa fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.
To view the fees for other available additional services, please, click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ukr/en/grc/additional-services).

## Documents Required
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photography). If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection
General information
- The customer is advised to provide all the necessary documentation in original. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.
- Please note that written consent from the parent/parentsnot travelling with the child must be submitted in original only. Certified copies are not accepted, the submitted original consent remains in the archive of the Consular Section and is not returned. To cross the border you will also need to provide original parental consent, so please pre-arrange receiving two original versions of this document from the notary office.
- Additional documentation may be requested by the Embassy of Greece.
- The following documents do not require translation or apostil.
- All submitted original documents except the passports remain in the archive of the Consular Section and are not returned.
General requirements:
1. Application form
- Completed and signed by the customer. Name and surname must be indicated in Latin letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer. To download application form, please, visit the section <>.
- The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
- Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
- Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. To download Agreement, please, visit the section Download Form.
- For your convenience Visa Application Centre offers the additional service of visa application form filling. The cost of the service is 85 UAH . You only need to provide information to the VAC employee who will fill in the data into an electronic form and attach a print-out to the supporting documents.
2. 1 Photograph
- In color, 3,5x4,5 cm
- Facing forward, white background
- Not older than 6 months before the submission date
- More detailed information can be found in the <<Photo specifications>> section
- o You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5x4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.
3. International passport(s)
- The passport must satisfy all criteria enlisted in art 12 of the Visa Code. Must be valid at least 3 months after the planned trip. Passport must contain 2 blank pages marked <<visas>>.
- If a child is travelling with a parent's passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all the pages of international passport except the blank ones.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - applicants under 18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
4. Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport except the blank ones.
5. Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for custemers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.
- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.
6. Minors that are travelling
- Written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child. The written consent must have a stamp of a Notary public and has to be presented in original. If there is only one custodian, a birth certificate, the court decision on sole custody, death certificate or other document must be presented to prove this. If the mother is entitled to sole custody in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine, you must provide a certificate from the Registry Office that a mother is raising a child alone in accordance with Art. 135 of the Family Code of Ukraine.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - if one/both parents have a valid Schengen visa and do not apply for a Greek Schengen visa, it is necessary to provide a written consent where the parent/parents give their permission for a child to travel and apply for a visa.
- Copies of all pages of the international and national passport of both parents (except the blank ones).
- Work certificates and financial documents of both parents
- Birth certificate - original and copy.
7. Tickets and accommodation
- Reservation of tickets and booking of a hotel.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
8. Proof of activity
- For employed persons: Employment certificate confirming the position of employee, the salary gained within the previous period of 6 months, the date of employment, and the duration of the vacation,
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - a copy of the work book must be provided.
- For private entrepreneurs:
- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company's payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners:
 Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- For pupils:
 Original certificate from the educational institution, which states that it is issued for submission to the Embassy of Greece.
- For students:
- Certificate from the university indicating the faculty, curriculum and graduation date, as well as the fact that the certificate is issued to the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine;
- A copy of the student card, certified by the university;
- A copy of the record book, certified by the university
9. Proof of sufficient financial means
- A bank account statement for the last 3 months and a copy of the bank card,
- Please, note that the minimum amount of funds in the account - 50 euros per day of stay in Greece for one person.
Sponsorship
- Expenses may be covered by the host or other third persons. The document that proves other person covering such expenses shall be a statement approved by public Notary and proof of income of the sponsor.
- Sponsorship letter for the family members (husband, wife, children) may be hand-written or typed, at your discretion. You must also provide proof of family relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate). For people who are not relatives, (husband, wife or children), sponsorship letter must be notarized. In both cases, the sponsorship letters should be complemented by a copy of the information page of internal or foreign passport and a certificate, proof of solvency of the sponsor (certificate of employment with an extract of wages for the last 6 months and a certificate from the bank on the movement of funds in the account for the last 3 months).
- You can find an example of the sponsorship letter for family members here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Sponsorship_letter_031015.pdf).
10. Official documents of the medical institutions
- Doctor's declaration/statement (the doctor is chosen by the Embassy) or/and the declaration/statement of a medical center/hospital, according to which the allegedly necessary medical treatment is not available in the customer's country of origin.
Confirming the necessity of medical care in this institution and making a prognosis of the medical case, including the duration of stay and cost estimation of the treatment they will be subjected to.
11. Proof of sufficient financial means to cover the costs of the planned treatment and cost of living.
- If cost of treatment or living is prepaid by applicant, then document confirming such payment.
Persons accompanying the customer will apply for a Schengen visa under the visa category of the "Private visit".
12. For the customers travelling with their families:
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5 * 4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.

## Processing Time
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ==) option to find out current status of your application.
VFS Global has no say in visa decisions taken by Embassy and Consulate General of Greece in Kiev and Odessa.

The service is purely optional and the customers may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH 85 (all taxes included).

Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.

You can find an example of filled in visa application form here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/aitisiukrainan_040815.pdf )
The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents) 
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. Please click here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/new-agrement-pilgrimage.pdf) to download the blank of the Personal Data Information Agreement

If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Pilgrimage]
Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined by the Visa Code of EU. All paid visa fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.
