## Documents Required
General information
- The customer is advised to provide all the necessary documentation in original. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.
- Please note that written consent from the parent/parents not travelling with the child must be submitted in original only. Certified copies are not accepted, the submitted original consent remains in the archive of the Consular Section and is not returned. To cross the border you will also need to provide original parental consent, so please pre-arrange receiving two original versions of this document from the notary office.
- Additional documentation may be requested by the Embassy of Greece.
- The following documents do not require translation or apostil.
- All submitted original documents except the passports remain in the archive of the Consular Section and are not returned.
General requirements:
1. Application form
- Completed and signed by the customer. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer. To download application form, please, visit the section <<Download Form>>.
- The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
- Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
- Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. To download Agreement, please, visit the section Download Form.
- For your convenience Visa Application Centre offers the additional service of visa application form filling. The cost of the service is UAH 85. You only need to provide information to the VAC employee who will fill in the data into an electronic form and attach a print-out to the supporting documents.
2. 1 Photograph
- In color, 3,5x4,5 cm
- Facing forward, white background
- Not older than 6 months before the submission date
- More detailed information can be found in the <<Photo specifications>> section
- You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5x4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.
3. International passport(s)
- The passport must satisfy all criteria enlisted in art 12 of the Visa Code. Must be valid at least 3 months after the planned trip. Passport must contain 2 blank pages marked <<visas>>.
- If a child is travelling with a parent's passport, 2 additional pages are required for a visa. Also, please, note that children aged 5 or older must have their photo in the passport.
- The customer must also present all other valid passports in original and, if available, the expired ones (not older than 3 years).
- Please, note that copies of previous visas can be accepted only if the original is provided.
- The customer must submit copies of all the pages of international passport except the blank ones.
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - customers under 18 must provide a separate travel document of a child.
4. Internal passport
- The customer must present the original and submit a copy of all pages of the internal passport except the blank ones.
5. Travel medical insurance
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of the intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 EUR in accordance with the art. 15.2 of the Visa Code, for customers receiving visas for multiple entries, the insurance only needs to cover the first trip.
- Please, note that you have the opportunity to buy a travel medical insurance directly inside the Visa Application Centre. Please consult the representative of the Insurance Company present at the Visa Application Centre for more details and cost of the travel insurance policy.
6. Tickets and accommodation
- Reservation of tickets.
- If the customers are travelling by car - a detailed travel plan, copy of driver license, certificate of car ownership and insurance of the vehicle.
- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
7. Proof of the purpose of the trip
- Email or fax confirmation of submission of the application for a permit to visit the Holy Mountain Athos, issued by the official office of pilgrims in Thessaloniki;
- A copy of the permit to visit the monastery;
8. Proof of activity
- For employed persons: Employment certificate confirming the position of employee, the salary gained within the previous period of 6 months, the date of employment, and the duration of the vacation,
- For those customers who submit their applications in Visa Application Centers under jurisdiction of General Consulate of Greece in Odessa - a copy of the work book must be provided.
- For private entrepreneurs:
- A copy of the certificate of registration of the private company;
- Original bank account statement of the private company account for the last 3 months;
- Original certificate from the State Tax Administration from the place of registration proving company's payment of the single tax.
- For pensioners:
 Pension certificate, the certificate of amount of the pension gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- For pupils:
 Original certificate from the educational institution, which states that it is issued for submission to the Embassy of Greece.
- For students:
- Certificate from the university indicating the faculty, curriculum and graduation date, as well as the fact that the certificate is issued to the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine;
- A copy of the student card, certified by the university;
- A copy of the record book, certified by the university

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5 * 4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.

## Processing Time
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ== ) option to find out current status of your application.
VFS Global has no say in visa decisions taken by Embassy and Consulate General of Greece in Kiev and Odessa.

Forms listed below must be filled in completely and must be signed by the customer himself/herself. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the
                                                                            international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer.
You can find an example of filled in visa application form here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/aitisiukrainan_040815.pdf )
The application forms of minor customers must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents) 
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms
                                                                            are also available at the Visa Application Centers. Please click here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/new-agrement-pilgrimage.pdf )                                                                            to download the blank of the Personal Data Information Agreement
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge).

- If a family is travelling, every member of the family has to present reservations with the list of all travelling family members.
- For the customers travelling with their families:
- Copies of all marriage and birth certificates to prove family ties (these documents should be presented by every family member)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses,
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
You have the opportunity to use the photo booth service in all the Visa Centers of the Embassy of Greece in Ukraine. Directly inside of the Visa Centers there are paid self-service coin operated vending machines. You can see the photographs taken on their screen and choose the best variant. The vending machine is very simple to use, and you can receive ready photos during 45 seconds. The nominal cost of the 4 photos of the passport format (3,5 * 4,5 cm) is 60 UAH.

## Processing Time
In practice, visa application processing takes up to 3 working days, depending on the number of applications, plus 1-5 days for visa application and passport delivery, depending on the destination. As a general rule, according to the Schengen Regulation, once a Schengen visa application is submitted to the Consulate, a decision is taken by the Consulate within 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to 30 days or 60 days. For detailed information please click here (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1484819697119&uri=CELEX:32009R0810).
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
Please, use Track my application (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/Global-Passporttracking/Track/Index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/E6yoKffIDSrH+PBHuJco2YGlskcMSmeAWPo3ioBR0ElmzOfiRv73RxnB1YLfwmtgQ== ) option to find out current status of your application.
VFS Global has no say in visa decisions taken by Embassy and Consulate General of Greece in Kiev and Odessa.

## Download Forms
Effective 26 May 2014 the Visa application Centre introduced the Form Filling service for the convenience of the customers at the centre.

The service is purely optional and the customers may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH 85 (all taxes included).

Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.

Forms listed below must be filled in completely and must be signed by the customer himself/herself. Name and surname must be indicated in Roman letters as they are written in the international passport, other information may be written in English, Greek, Ukrainian or Russian. The form can be filled either manually or using computer.

 You can find an example of filled in visa application form here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/aitisiukrainan_040815.pdf )The application forms of minor applicants must be signed by the person entitled to custody (as a rule, parents)
                                                                        
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Please download and fill in the form of the Personal Data Processing Agreement. The Personal Data Processing Agreement is required to be signed personally by customers, the forms are also available at the Visa Application Centers. Please click here (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/english/pdf/new-agrement-pilgrimage.pdf) to download the blank of the Personal Data Information Agreement
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge).

Visa Application Form (https://www.greecevisa-ukraine.com/pdf/aitisiukrainan.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Drivers conducting international cargo and passenger transportation services to Greece in vehicles registered in Ukraine.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
To optimize the process of documents submission, please, use the following check-list (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/greece/ukraine/english/pdf/Checklist_Drivers_271115.pdf). Using it, you can check if you have all the required documents to submit according to the chosen visa category and arrange the documents in the required order. You can find detailed requirements to every point of the check-list in the "Documents Required" section.
Visa Application processing takes up to 4 working days, extended up to 15 working days according to the Schengen Regulations, depending on the number of applications submitted and the VIS.
Visa applications shall be lodged no more than three (3) months prior to the intended date of visit.
Please, note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer at the Consular Office of Greece prior to a decision being taken on your application.
In order to submit the documents, customers must visit the Visa Application Centers in person and provide biometric data (fingerprints and digital photograph).
If an customer is exempted from biometric data collection, the application may be submitted by the close relatives (children, parents, husband/wife, grandparents, grandchildren) (providing an authorization letter and the documents confirming family ties) or representatives of accredited travel agencies. For the customers, who are going to submit their documents in Visa Application Center in Odessa - application may be submitted by the third person with a notarized authorization letter.
Visa Application Centres working hours are: 08.30 - 15.30 (Monday to Friday except weekends and holidays).
Visa applications could be submitted at the visa application centers in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipro, Chernivtsi and Lutsk. Applications submitted in these locations will be assessed by the Embassy of Greece in Kiev. Visa applications could be submitted at the Visa Application Center in Odessa. Applications submitted here will be assessed by the Consulate General of Greece in Odessa.
Information regarding the regional jurisdiction of the Consular Departments of the Embassy of Greece and the addresses of nearby Visa Application Centres can be found in the section "Contacts".
